title: Node Incentives
summary: A lesson on why participant behavior needs aligned incentives if confidential execution is going to remain durable and useful.

bodySections.title: Behavior Follows Incentives
bodySections.body:
Networks are not just software. They are collections of participants whose behavior is shaped by rewards, constraints, and opportunity cost.

If incentives are misaligned, even a technically elegant system can become unreliable in practice.

bodySections.title: Durability
bodySections.body:
Durability comes from making the desirable behavior economically rational often enough that the network keeps functioning under real-world pressure.

That is why incentive design belongs in the same conversation as architecture and security.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why does the lesson say incentives matter to network behavior?
How is durability described in this lesson?
choices: As making desired behavior economically rational often enough to keep the network functioning
As maximizing inflation regardless of results
